ogTitle: Pewsey Vale vs Piccadilly: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Pewsey Vale and Piccadilly.
heading: Pewsey Vale vs Piccadilly — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Pewsey Vale, South Australia and Piccadilly, South Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Pewsey Vale has a lower overall climate risk score (32/100) compared to Piccadilly (37/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Pewsey Vale or Piccadilly safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Pewsey Vale has a flood risk score of 30/100, while Piccadilly scores 39/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Pewsey Vale scores 26/100 for bushfire risk. Piccadilly scores 26/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Pewsey Vale or Piccadilly?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Pewsey Vale has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
